summary: hipEngine resident c=1 now uses parent-mixed FP16 activations/scratch/wrappers with BF16 KV and q/k head-norm exceptions, but the 512-token parent fixture still fails first-token parity.

commands.guard: python3 -m compileall -q hipengine tests scripts && python3 -m pytest tests/test_qwen35_decode_state.py tests/test_qwen35_paro_layout.py tests/test_loading_materialize.py tests/test_generation_qwen35_paro.py tests/test_runtime_workspace.py tests/test_qwen35_resident_batch_layout.py tests/test_generation_batch_scheduler.py -q

fixes_validated: resident embedding/hidden/scratch/final-norm paths switched to FP16 activations
linear-attention, full-attention, MoE, router, dense GEMV, PARO SiLU/rotate/combine, and W8A16 lowp wrappers now have FP16 resident dispatch paths
full-attention KV remains BF16 while paged KV write accepts FP16 value inputs
final norm emits FP16 and casts to BF16 before the temporary BF16 lm-head path
blockers: Parent fixture first decode token still mismatches after parent-mixed FP16 switch: expected 1739, got 58 with top logit 9.434697151184082.
The HIP prefill sampled seed changed to 220 in this run; next task should bisect the residual parity gap at per-layer logits/hidden states instead of broad BF16-vs-FP16 activation policy.
hipEngine prefill remains sequential c=1 and is not parent bulk/native compact prefill speed-equivalent.
Observed tok/s are correctness-smoke timings only and are not retained performance claims because correctness failed.
